title: Submission Record Series: BatchOpt+MLP4+RoPE100k and 8L EMA Int6 Bigram65k on Single 20GB GPU (val_bpb 1.7810 → 1.3092)
author: markste-in
body:
This MR bundles two consecutive improvements for the 10min/16MB track, all developed and validated on a single small GPU (20GB VRAM) under the 600s wall-clock constraint.

1) 2026-03-22 — BatchOpt + MLP4 + RoPE100k

Improved val_bpb from 1.7810 → 1.4784 (+16.9%).
Main drivers: batch reduction (196k → 98k tokens) to increase update count, MLP_MULT=4, LR retune (0.035), shorter warmdown (600), and ROPE_BASE=100k.
Result: strong quality gain while staying comfortably within size/runtime limits.
2) 2026-03-23 — 8L + EMA + Int6-MLP + BigramHash-65k

Further improved to 1.3092 (1.3978 → 1.3092 in session-2, +6.3%).
Main drivers: 8-layer architecture, BigramHash vocab scaled to 65k, EMA replacing SWA, longer linear warmdown (3000), stride-64 sliding eval, int6 MLP quantization, and 1% magnitude pruning.
Final artifact: 15.93MB (within 16MB cap).
Net effect: a staged progression from baseline 1.7810 to final 1.3092, achieved with practical single-GPU (20GB) optimization rather than large-cluster training.
